assumption_text: The episode footprint is the encircled West Beirut urban area from 14 June to 21 August 1982, excluding East Beirut.
rationale: This is the most explicit civilian-denominator footprint in the source base and is repeatedly described as the encircled western half of the city.

assumption_text: Average exposed civilian population is modeled at 440,000-500,000 based on a 500,000 baseline and a documented 30,000-60,000 outflow.
rationale: Time gives the trapped baseline; Adelman gives explicit flight from West Beirut.

assumption_text: Average organized armed personnel in the siege theater are modeled at 6,000-10,000, with the upper scenario adding some PLA/Syrian personnel back into a PLO-centered base estimate.
rationale: Time and Sayigh both support a core 6,000-7,000 PLO presence; Sayigh also notes excluded Syrian/PLA personnel.

assumption_text: The 84 percent Beirut civilian-share figure is applied to mixed Beirut total-death series to recover civilian and military deaths for the core estimate path.
rationale: No retrieved source publishes a full exact-episode civilian/military/unknown split for West Beirut; this case-specific allocation is more transparent than leaving the core metric unestimated.

assumption_text: Person-months are computed from a 69-day siege duration using a simple days/30 conversion.
rationale: Daily headcount series are unavailable, so person-month exposure is approximated from the bounded episode dates.

assumption_text: Inflicting-side fields are left null for the core West Beirut estimates because the full-episode casualty series generally describe deaths from the siege or from all sides, not a consistent actor-specific perpetrator count.
rationale: The side-attribution rule forbids inferring an attacker identity when the source does not state it explicitly.
